rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture.
Place component type annexes in valid component type scopes and implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes.
Use visible identifiers and avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts.
Align AGREE scalar types with expression types to avoid type mismatches.
generic_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks.
Use valid component type scopes for component type annexes and implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings.
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts.
Align AGREE scalar types with expression types to prevent type mismatches.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use AGREE scalar types int, real, and bool for declarations.
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope.
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts.
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons.
Do not place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes without concrete bounds or visible relations.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
